read_modify_write_races.pattern: Module-level let lastTimestamp=0, counter=0 — non-atomic counter increment under concurrent create() calls within same millisecond
read_modify_write_races.detail: Two concurrent create() calls with the same Date.now() will both read lastTimestamp === currentTimestamp (true), then counter++ is NOT atomic. Both can read the same counter value, producing duplicate IDs for events, sessions, messages. This corrupts global event ordering and can cause FK constraint violations in storage.

read_modify_write_races.pattern: Module-level Map<string, MachineDef>() and Map<string, MachineRunner>() mutated via Effect.sync() without Ref — non-atomic set/delete under concurrent spawn/cancel
read_modify_write_races.detail: machines.set(def.id, def) and runners.set(laneId, runner) are wrapped in Effect.sync which is synchronous — but concurrent fibers calling spawn() and cancel() will interleave on the Map. cancel() could delete a runner that another fiber just created via spawn() if the fiber scheduling interleaves the sync calls.

read_modify_write_races.pattern: data.runners.get(sessionID) check-then-act: InstanceState.get(state) returns mutable Map ref, then code does .get() + .set()/delete() in separate Effect steps
read_modify_write_races.detail: InstanceState.get(state) returns a reference to the internal state. Subsequent .get() and .set()/delete() calls on data.runners are not atomic — two concurrent fibers for the same sessionID could both pass the 'if (existing) return existing' guard and create duplicate Runner instances. See line 55-60 (runner function) where delete happens in onIdle callback.

read_modify_write_races.pattern: Ref.get(store) + subsequent logic + later Ref.update(store) — read-modify-write not atomic
read_modify_write_races.detail: Multiple patterns in binder.ts follow Ref.get(store) then later Ref.update(store, ...). If the store changes between get and update, the update's closure captures stale state. Should use Ref.modify for atomic read-modify-write.

check_then_act_races.pattern: Filesystem.exists(filepath) → Filesystem.readText(filepath) — TOCTOU in ACP server permission handler
check_then_act_races.detail: Classic TOCTOU race in the ACP server's permission reply path. Filesystem.exists wraps sync existsSync (blocks event loop). Between the exists check and readText call, the file can be deleted. readText error is unhandled, which causes an uncaught promise rejection. This runs on the concurrent multi-request ACP server path.

check_then_act_races.pattern: existsSync(file) check then read — TOCTOU race on filesystem
check_then_act_races.detail: existsSync(file) at line 344 checks if config file exists, then reads it. File could be deleted between check and read. Since this is sync (blocks event loop), the window is smaller but real if another process deletes the file. Similarly at line 449 (check then write) — seed config could be overwritten by concurrent reload — and line 460 (check then migrate legacy file).

check_then_act_races.pattern: existsSafe → git config → fs.remove — multi-step TOCTOU with concurrent clone race
check_then_act_races.detail: Multiple existsSafe checks at lines 204-211 followed by git operations and fs.remove. Between check and clone, another process could have started cloning. Flock is imported but not used on this path.

check_then_act_races.pattern: Effect.promise(() => fs.access) → Effect.promise(() => fs.readFile) — TOCTOU with unhandled ENOENT
check_then_act_races.detail: Two separate Effect.promise blocks: first checks file exists (access), then reads it (readFile). If file is deleted between access and readFile, the second Effect.promise throws an unhandled error.

check_then_act_races.pattern: existsSafe(plan) check then read/write plan file — TOCTOU race
check_then_act_races.detail: Line 54 checks if plan file exists then uses result as prompt context. Line 73-74 checks existence then creates dir + writes plan — file could be created by another session between check and write.

check_then_act_races.pattern: existsSafe check then writeFileString — TOCTOU on writes
check_then_act_races.detail: existsSafe(filePath) at line 32 checks if file existed (for logging), then writeFileString at line 34 writes. Two concurrent writes to same path will interleave/corrupt the file.

check_then_act_races.pattern: existsSafe → spawnSync('chmod') — TOCTOU on binary files
check_then_act_races.detail: existsSafe checks if binary exists locally at lines 141, 167, 178. Between check and spawnSync('chmod') or download, the binary could be deleted by another process. Unhandled effect failure.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: Read-modify-write on docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.json — 3 tools contend without lock
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: roadmap-progress.ts:44, roadmap-prioritize.ts:40, and roadmap-deprecate.ts:39 ALL read, modify in memory, and write back to the same file. Two concurrent operations race: both read the original, modify independently, one write silently overwrites the other's changes. No lock or coordination. Same file is a hot contention point.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: Read-modify-write on shared JSONL progress file — read entire file, modify, write back
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: tool-feedback.ts:88 reads the full JSONL file into memory, modifies it, and writes back atomically via writeFileString. This is actually a read-modify-write on JSONL — worse than plain append. Concurrent calls lose entries.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: Direct writeFile(p, content) without temp-file+rename — not atomic
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: The central write() function uses await writeFile(p, content) directly. No atomic write pattern (write to .tmp then rename). If two concurrent fibers write to the same path, the content will be interleaved at the filesystem level. This affects ALL callers: smart-write, smart-sd, tool-feedback, log-activity, publish-finding, etc.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: Append-write to shared JSONL file (flag: 'a') without file lock
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: Multiple tools (tool-feedback.ts, log-activity.ts, publish-finding.ts, qa-observed-clean.ts, record-stress-wave.ts, record-execution-wave.ts) write to JSONL files with { flag: 'a' }. Two concurrent appends from different fibers can produce interleaved JSON lines.
concurrent_file_write_risks.witness: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool-feedback.ts:88,131; log-activity.ts:54; publish-finding.ts:109; qa-observed-clean.ts:54; record-stress-wave.ts:66; record-execution-wave.ts:71; smart-bash.ts:114; smart-sd.ts:124; replace-symbol.ts:77

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: Append-write to JSONL progress file without file lock
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: Uses writeFileString + { flag: 'a' } for roadmap progress log. Concurrent tool calls can interleave writes.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: fsNode.writeFile direct write to config.json without atomic write
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: Line 468 writes config.json directly. If process crashes mid-write, the config file is truncated/corrupt. Line 828 uses writeFileString on config file.

concurrent_file_write_risks.pattern: writeFileString to report path without atomic write
concurrent_file_write_risks.detail: Multiple writeFileString calls at lines 71, 82, 91 write reports. Concurrent generation of same report type could corrupt.

global_state_issues.detail: Three module-level let vars and two Maps (pendingAuths, mcpNameToState) with NO synchronization. Two concurrent ensureRunning() calls race on server creation — both check 'if (server) return', both proceed, one server replaces the other. The old server leaks. Concurrent waitForCallback() and handleRequest() race on pendingAuths Map.

Module-level Map<string, TransportWithAuth>() shared across all MCP operations without synchronization. Transport can be created, stored, retrieved, and removed from different Effect fibers. No lock or Ref wrapping.

Module-level Map<string, Definition>() registry, Map<string, number>() versions, let projectors, let frozen, let convertEvent — all mutable global state. registry.set() called by init() and registry.get() called by subscribers. No synchronization. The 'frozen' flag and 'convertEvent' function pointer are also unsafe.

Module-level Map<ProjectID, Map<string, WorkspaceAdapter>>() — registerAdapter() does check-then-act pattern: state.get(projectID) ?? new Map() then state.set(projectID, adapters). Two concurrent registerAdapter calls for the same projectID can both create a new Map and one overwrites the other, losing one adapter registration.

Module-level Map<string, Explanation>() cache. clearExplanationCache() calls cache.clear() in Effect.sync. invalidateExplanation() calls cache.delete() in Effect.sync. explainEvent() reads cache.get() in Effect context. Concurrent deletes and reads on the same Map from different fibers is a read-modify race on the Map's internal structure.
Module-level Bonjour service reference and port. No synchronization on start/stop. Concurrent start calls could create duplicate service advertisements.
Module-level Map that is lazily initialized. Access pattern: if (!projectors) projectors = new Map() — two concurrent accessors can both initialize, one losing the other's data.

queue_overflows.pattern: PubSub.unbounded() — no backpressure on core event bus
queue_overflows.detail: PubSub.unbounded() creates an unbounded queue. Any slow subscriber leaks memory with no bound. The event bus at core/src/event.ts:91,99 is the central backbone — a single slow consumer causes unbounded memory growth. Same pattern at core/src/plugin.ts:120, opencode/src/bus/index.ts:68,94, context/invalidation-bus.ts:24,39. Recommend PubSub.sliding() or PubSub.bounded().

queue_overflows.pattern: cancelBackgroundJobs while-loop with unbounded fibers inside — multiplicative DoS vector
queue_overflows.detail: cancelBackgroundJobs spawns unbounded Effect.forEach fibers (line 143) inside a while loop that re-filters the same job list. If background.list() returns enough jobs, each iteration spawns unlimited cancel fibers. The while loop can iterate multiple times per session cancel. This is the most dangerous single pattern found.

Unbounded concurrency with 'discard: true' silently drops tasks exceeding system capacity at 26 production sites. Hot paths include: session.ts:946, session/processor.ts:751, session/llm.ts:102, session/prompt.ts:236, session/run-state.ts:41,143, file/ripgrep.ts:245,402, mcp/index.ts:595,615,762,776, tool/registry.ts:738, tool/read.ts:87.

queue_overflows.pattern: Capacity-bounded ring buffer using Ref.update — correct bounded behavior
queue_overflows.detail: Flight recorder properly drops oldest events when capacity is reached. Uses Ref.update for atomicity. This is the correct pattern for bounded buffers.

queue_overflows.pattern: Cache.make with bounded capacity + TTL — correct bounded behavior
queue_overflows.detail: PTY ticket cache uses Cache.make with explicit capacity and timeToLive. Evicts old entries. Correct bounded pattern.

queue_overflows.pattern: Cache.make with capacity: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY — effectively unbounded
queue_overflows.detail: Account cache has infinite capacity but likely bounded by number of accounts. Still a risk if account tokens cycle frequently.

pre_existing_findings_relevant.detail: Previously discovered: cache.ts has concurrent dedup race where under concurrent load, compute failures cause fiber leaks and concurrent requests for same key wastefully duplicate computation. Aligns with audit finding that Ref.modify not used for inflight dedup.
Prepublication review records with premature positive verdicts — governance timing race. Not directly a code concurrency issue but a process one.

critical_issues: packages/opencode/src/id/id.ts:19 — Non-atomic counter increment in ID generation can produce DUPLICATE IDs across concurrent create() calls. HIGHEST RISK: duplicate event/session/message IDs corrupt event ordering.

packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.ts:9 — Module-level let vars + Maps with ZERO synchronization. Concurrent ensureRunning() calls create duplicate HTTP servers. pendingAuths Map races under concurrent waitForCallback + callback handler.

packages/opencode/src/util/filesystem.ts:60 — Central write() function uses direct writeFile() without atomic write (no temp+rename). ALL file writes in the codebase are non-atomic. Concurrent writes to same path produce interleaved/corrupt output. Affects all JSONL append targets.
packages/opencode/src/control-plane/adapters/index.ts:37 — registerAdapter() has check-then-act on module-level Map. Concurrent registration for same projectID loses one adapter.
packages/opencode/src/sync/index.ts:206 — Module-level registry Map + frozen flag + function pointer with no synchronization. Concurrent event type registration loses definitions.
packages/opencode/src/agent/machine/runtime.ts:222 — Machine/runner Maps mutated via Effect.sync without Ref. Concurrent spawn+cancel races on the Maps.
packages/opencode/src/event/explanation.ts:50 — Module-level cache Map accessed concurrently from Effect fibers without synchronization.
packages/opencode/src/session/run-state.ts:55 — Non-atomic get+set/delete on InstanceState-managed runners Map. Two fibers for same sessionID can create duplicate Runner instances.
packages/opencode/src/tool/tool-feedback.ts:88 — Multiple tools append to shared JSONL files with no file lock. Concurrent appends from different fibers produce interleaved JSON.
packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts:344 — existsSync TOCTOU race on config file. File deleted between check and read causes crash.

solidjs_store_analysis.verdict: Generally disciplined. All store array mutations use produce() or setter callbacks. Race conditions guarded with inflight maps, scope re-checks, and tracked() guards.

medium_risk.pattern: createStore captures initial signal values (todos(), live()) once — stale first render for `dock` before effect runs
medium_risk.detail: The store initializer reads reactive signals synchronously during createStore. If signals change before the first createEffect runs, `dock` shows wrong state on initial render. Self-correcting but produces a visible flash.

clean_patterns: All produce() usage in event-reducer.ts (17 call sites) wraps every draft.splice()/push()
directory-sync.ts uses inflight map + tracked() guard for loadMessages race prevention
tree-store.ts uses inflight map + scope re-check to prevent stale directory listDir results
session-history-loader.ts uses stale-ID guard after each async await

verified_safe: packages/opencode/src/acp-next/session.ts — All operations use Ref.modify/Ref.update atomically on Ref-wrapped Map. SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/debug/flight-recorder.ts — Ref.update with capacity check. Ref.getAndSet for flush. SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/registry.ts — Ref.update for all Map mutations. SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/campaign/orchestrator.ts — Uses Ref properly (Ref.get + Ref.update patterns). SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/campaign/secretary.ts — Ref.update for all activeLanes mutations. SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/server/health.ts — Ref.get and Ref.update on Ref-wrapped state. SAFE.
packages/opencode/src/lifecycle/gate.ts — Ref.set/Ref.get on Ref-wrapped state (single-writer pattern). SAFE.
recommendations: P1: Wrap id/id.ts counter in a Semaphore or use Ref.modify for atomic counter increment
P1: Add file-level lock (e.g., flock on fd, or a Mutex/Ref guard) for all JSONL append-write targets
P1: Use atomic write pattern (write to .tmp, then rename) in filesystem.ts write()
P2: Wrap all module-level mutable Maps in Ref (sync/index.ts, control-plane/adapters/index.ts, event/explanation.ts, mcp/oauth-callback.ts)
P2: Replace Effect.sync(Map.set) calls in agent/machine/runtime.ts with Effect.acquireRelease or Ref-based mutations
P2: Audit all Effect.forEach with { concurrency: 'unbounded' } and add bounded limits where possible
P3: Replace existsSync/existsSafe + subsequent file ops with try/catch error handling (no check-then-act)
P3: Replace session/run-state.ts InstanceState.get() + .get().set() with atomic InstanceState.modify() if available
P3: Add guard to ensureRunning() in oauth-callback.ts to prevent duplicate HTTP server creation
